title: cynara palustris
intro: Cynara palustris, also known as marsh artichoke or marsh thistle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing cholesterol levels, improving digestion, and protecting against liver and kidney damage. Cynara palustris is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
